query: we decided to use patient-level splitting instead of image-level splitting to prevent data leakage — the 80/20 split with seed 42 gives us cleaner test results
I chose ResNet-50 over VGG-16 because it has better parameter efficiency and the pretrained weights from ImageNet transfer well to our chest X-ray domain
after reviewing the learning rate sweep results, 0.001 gives the best validation AUC at 0.94 — 0.01 caused instability after epoch 15 and 0.0001 was too slow
we're going to reuse the existing Disease_Classification feature rather than creating a new Diagnosis feature — it already has 3,200 values and fragmenting would cause confusion
I decided to add the Enrollment_Date column to the existing Subject table instead of creating a new Patient table, since Subject already has 1,247 records and 8 incoming FKs
we picked cifar10_small_labeled_split over cifar10_small_split because the test partition needs ground truth labels for ROC analysis — the unlabeled split has no test labels
I'm choosing dropout 0.25 and weight decay 1e-4 for the extended model because the quick model showed clear overfitting by epoch 30 with no regularization
after testing both stratified random split and group-by-patient split on dataset 2-B4C8, we went with group-by-patient to avoid multiple images from the same patient appearing in both train and test
we decided to create a separate Augmented dataset rather than adding augmented samples to the original training set, so we can cleanly compare augmented vs non-augmented results
I chose to use 3 epochs for the quick config because it's enough to verify the pipeline works without wasting compute — the extended 50-epoch config is for final results
I need to run the cifar10_quick experiment with dry_run=true to test the pipeline
